content.full_text:
Re:
Farn PolrrrcAr- PRACTTcES C onaurssroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
June 3,2005
Elliott Robinson, Director
Monterey County
Department of Social and Employment
Services
1000 South Main Street
Salinas, CA 93901
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-05-062
Dear Ms. Robinson:
This letter is in response to your request on behalf of Monterey County, for advice
regarding the statements of economic interests provisions of the political Reform Act (the
"Act').1
QUESTION
Are mernbers of the Workforce Invesknent Board ('WIB') of Monterey County
public officials who must file Statements of Economic Interests?
CONCLUSION
Mernbers of the WIB of Monterey County, which will serve as a workforce
invest :ent board pursuant to the Workforce Investrnent Act, are public officials who
must file Statements of Economic Interests and are subject to the Act,s conflict-of-
interest rules"
I Governmert Code sections 81000 - gl0l4. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
grts


FileNo. A-05-062
Page No. 2
rACTS
The Federal Workforce Investment Act delegates local workforce investment
boards authority to certifu One-Stop Career Center operators, to award grants to youth
and adult employment service providers, and budget development. In Monterey CountS
members of the Workforce Investrnent Board ("WB') are appointed by the Board of
Supervisors to implement the requirements of Workforce Investrnent Act (*WIA') and to
develop strategic plans for workforce development, monitor local programs and
recommend funding for service delivery. The WIB is staffed by Monterey County
employees and the county acts as fiscal agent through the County's Deparfinent of Social
and Employment Services.
Consistent with Federal law, Monterey County WIB activities include:
recommendation of budgets to the Board of Supervisors, preparation and evaluation of
requests for proposals, recofllmendation of contract awards to the Board of Supenrisors,
and monitoring ofprogram performance. The decisions of the WIB require concrurerrce
by the Board of Supervisors in order to be final.
ANALYSIS
Section 82003 defines "agency''to include "any state or local govemment
agency." Section 82Ul defines "local government agenct'' to mean "a county, city or
district of any kind including school district, or any other local orregional political
subdivision, or any department, division, bureau, office, board, commission or other
agency of the foregoing."
We have previously advised that WIBs are local governmental agencies, and
thereforemust adopt a conflict of interest code. (Yig Rern Advice Letter, No. A-02-124;
Holland Advice Letter, No. I-01-140; Dostart Advice Letter, No. A-00-022.) Because
the WIB of Monterey County serves as a WIB, it is a local government agency for
purposes of the Act. As such, the menrbers sitting on a local WIB are "public officials"
under the Act.2 As public officials, all of the *e*bor of the WIB's board are subject to
the Act's conflict-of-interest rules since they will make or participate in the making of
governmental decisions. (Section 87100; Dostart, supra.)
Additionally, since the members will make or participate in the making of
governmental decisions, they will be "designated employees" subject to the Act's
disclosure rules and required to submit Statements of Economic Interests ("SEI's'). A
"designated employee" includes a mernber of an agency whose position with the agency
is designated in a conflict of interest code because the position entails the making or
participation in the making of decisions which may foreseeably have a material effect on
any financial interest. (Section 8201 9(a)(3).)
' "Public official" is defined as every member, officer, employee or consultant of a state or local
govemment agency, with certain exceptions not applicable here. (Section 82048.)


File No. A-05-62
Page No- 3
Please be aware that whether a separate code is established or the officials are
included in Monterey County's code is a determination to be made by the Board of
Supervisors, the code reviewing body. (Sections 87301and 82011; see also regulation
18329.5.) If you have any other questions regarding this matter or need technical
assistance regarding the establishment or amendment of a codg please contact the
Division of Technical Assistance at (916) 322-566A.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
By:
Galena West
Counsel, Legal Division
GW:rd
